summary: Cleaned and matched parts of the common fighter item throw implementation in src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_ItemThrow.c. The PR removed junk padding inlines and manual dummy stack arrays in favor of PAD_STACK, restored ftCo_80095700 as a normal static helper instead of force_active-pragmas, simplified heavy-throw motion selection logic, introduced a small getAnimSpeed inline, and converted several direct gobj->user_data fighter accesses to GET_FIGHTER. No review comments were present in the slice.
what_changed: Added include of ft/inlines.h, enabling use of project inline/helper macros such as GET_FIGHTER and PAD_STACK.
Removed the separate getHeavyThrowMsid helper and folded its forward/back heavy throw direction choice into ftCo_80094EA4 using stick_x * fp->facing_dir ternaries.

Refactored ftCo_80094EA4 to use clearer stick_x and stick_y names instead of generic var_f30/var_f31 roles, early-return when fp->item_gobj is NULL, and apply PAD_STACK(8) instead of a dummy u8 array.
Changed ftCo_80095700 from a force_active global-looking function bracketed by #pragma push/#pragma force_active on/#pragma pop into a static helper with a normal static prototype.
Replaced dummy stack arrays in ftCo_80095744, ftCo_800957F4, and ftCo_800958FC with explicit PAD_STACK calls.

Introduced inline float getAnimSpeed(ftCo_GObj* gobj, int msid), factoring the animation speed calculation out of ftCo_800957F4 while preserving use of p_ftCommonData->x400 for light throw motion IDs and inverse item speed from it_8026B334.
Simplified ftCo_800957F4 by assigning fp->mv.co.itemthrow4.anim_spd from getAnimSpeed, then passing the same anim_spd to Fighter_ChangeMotionState.

Collapsed two inline helpers, inlineA1 and inlineA0, into a single inlineA0 that performs the temporary facing_dir swap, motion-state change, accessory callback setup, and immediate ftCo_80095EFC call.

Converted several fighter pointer loads from gobj->user_data to GET_FIGHTER(gobj), including in ftCo_ItemThrow_Anim, ftCo_80095EFC, ftCo_LightThrowDash_Phys, inlineA0, ftCo_80096374, and ftCo_80096498.
Simplified ftCo_80096374 by using fp->motion_id directly in the branch instead of introducing a local msid variable.
smart_moves: Using PAD_STACK documents intentional stack shaping directly and removes misleading zero-initialized dummy arrays such as u8 _[8] = { 0 }, making the source easier to maintain while preserving matching-sensitive layout.
Replacing #pragma force_active with a normal static helper suggests the function is now naturally retained by references, reducing artificial compiler-control noise.

The getAnimSpeed inline isolates a repeated-looking expression in ftCo_800957F4 without changing the observable call sequence around ftCo_80095700, Fighter_ChangeMotionState, ftAnim_8006EBA4, and accessory callback setup.
Renaming throw-input temporaries to stick_x and stick_y makes ftCo_80094EA4's directional motion-ID decision tree much easier to audit.
Using GET_FIGHTER consistently aligns this file with common fighter-access conventions and can help matching when the macro expands in expected forms.
decomp_lessons: Prefer PAD_STACK(N) over ad hoc dummy byte arrays when the only purpose is matching stack size or layout.
If a force_active pragma was only compensating for earlier decomp structure, re-check whether normal static linkage now keeps the helper alive after call graph cleanup.
For input-heavy fighter code, meaningful locals like stick_x and stick_y reduce decomp risk compared with generic var_f names, especially when matching nested threshold checks.
Small inline extraction can improve readability and still match if the generated code preserves the original arithmetic order and local stack behavior.
Direct gobj->user_data loads and GET_FIGHTER(gobj) should be chosen deliberately; this PR shows a cleanup trend toward GET_FIGHTER in common fighter callbacks.
When decompiling directional motion selection, preserve signed comparisons involving facing_dir, such as stick_x * fp->facing_dir >= 0.0f, because they encode forward/back throw selection.
naming_conventions: Use stick_x and stick_y for fighter stick input components when those values drive directional motion selection.
Use msid for FtMotionId or motion state ID locals.
Use anim_spd for computed animation speed stored into fp->mv.co.itemthrow4.anim_spd and passed to Fighter_ChangeMotionState.
Use GET_FIGHTER(gobj) for fighter extraction in callbacks where that macro convention is accepted.
Helper names in this file remain mostly address-based ftCo_80095xxx where no semantic name is known; the PR did not rename address-based functions beyond inline helper cleanup.
The retained inline helper name inlineA0 is non-semantic and appears matching-driven; evidence for a better semantic name was not present.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Replaced dummy stack allocations with PAD_STACK(4), PAD_STACK(8), and PAD_STACK(16) to express required padding without fake variables.
Removed a junk inline getHeavyThrowMsid and used inline ternary expressions for heavy forward/back motion IDs, likely matching compiler output more directly.
Removed force_active pragmas around ftCo_80095700 after making it static and referenced normally.

Factored animation speed computation into an inline getAnimSpeed while retaining the multiplication by p_ftCommonData->x400 for msid >= ftCo_MS_LightThrowF4 and multiplication by 1 / it_8026B334(fp->item_gobj).
Merged inlineA1 into inlineA0 to avoid unnecessary inline layering while preserving the temporary facing direction change and restored facing_dir after Fighter_ChangeMotionState.
Kept a goto label block_32 in ftCo_80094EA4, indicating the control-flow shape was still important for matching the nested stick-angle decision tree.
follow_up_queries: Look for later PRs that semantically rename ftCo_80095700, ftCo_800957F4, or ftCo_800958FC.
Search for other ftCommon files replacing dummy u8 stack arrays with PAD_STACK to reuse the same matching tactic.
Compare other item throw callbacks for GET_FIGHTER adoption versus direct gobj->user_data access.
Check whether inlineA0 later receives a semantic name or is removed after further matching of item throw collision transitions.
